content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

All four amendments have technical merit. Two expose concrete replay/matching defects in `unique_terminal_replay_match`, and two identify real plan-hash provenance gaps where the code either records the latest snapshot hash instead of the dispatch plan or leaves legacy rows permanently unlabeled.

## Votes

- `fr-10-681307ea`: Accept
  The helper’s own contract says `(Some(run_id), None)` is unsafe because it disables the `started_at` guard for runless terminal candidates. `debug_assert!` vanishes in release builds, so production code no longer enforces that safety invariant. Current callers happen to pass `Some(started_at)`, but this is still a real robustness defect in a correctness-critical helper and should be enforced at runtime.
- `fr-10-fa9c76f6`: Accept
  The comment already recognizes that `snapshot.plan_hash` can advance after an attempt started, which is why existing rows are not backfilled from the snapshot. That same timing problem exists for newly created rows if dispatch happened under one plan version and `record_task_run_start` runs after a later `persist_plan`. In that case auto-population can stamp the wrong plan hash, so documenting the limitation or requiring callers to pass the known dispatch hash is justified.
- `fr-10-9757053d`: Accept
  The current repair/finalization paths only copy a caller-supplied `plan_hash` onto preexisting rows and otherwise intentionally leave `None` untouched. That avoids mislabeling, but it also means legacy or partial rows can never recover plan provenance, even when a safe backfill could be determined. The amendment correctly identifies this as an unresolved correctness/diagnostics gap and points at the right requirement: either add a safe backfill rule or persist enough provenance to know when backfill is valid.
- `fr-10-a0a290d3`: Accept
  In the named-run fallback path, `unique_terminal_replay_match()` receives every row for the same bead/project, and then requires every non-candidate row to be a legacy start paired with the candidate. That wrongly disqualifies a valid runless terminal for attempt A once unrelated attempt B has any completed row, even if `started_at` uniquely identifies A. Filtering the fallback set by attempt identity before running the uniqueness check is the correct fix.
